title: Warehouse Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the Warehouse Department.
Learn how materials are received, stored and issued.
Recognize the importance of stock accuracy.
Learn how SAP supports warehouse operations.

introduction.title: What is the Warehouse Department?
introduction.content: The Warehouse Department is responsible for receiving, storing, protecting and issuing raw materials, spare parts and finished goods. It ensures that materials are available in the right quantity, at the right place and at the right time to support uninterrupted business operations.

business_purpose.title: Why is the Warehouse Department Important?
business_purpose.content: An efficient warehouse minimizes production delays by maintaining accurate inventory, ensuring proper storage and enabling quick material movement. Good warehouse management reduces losses, improves inventory visibility and supports timely customer deliveries.

warehouse_workflow: Inspect received materials with Quality (if required).

key_responsibilities.description: Receive and verify incoming materials from suppliers.
Store materials safely using designated storage locations.
Maintain accurate stock records and inventory levels.
Issue materials to Production and Maintenance against approved requests.
Store approved finished products until dispatch.
Conduct periodic physical stock verification and reconciliation.

inventory_management_principles: FEFO (First Expired, First Out) where applicable

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Issues raw materials required for manufacturing.
Supplies spare parts and maintenance materials.
Coordinates inspection of incoming and finished goods.
Transfers finished goods for customer shipment.
Provides inventory valuation and stock reports.

business_example.title: Warehouse Operations at JSL
business_example.content: A truck carrying stainless steel coils arrives at the plant. Warehouse personnel verify the delivery documents and record the Goods Receipt in SAP MM. After Quality approves the material, it is stored in the designated location. When Production raises a material request, Warehouse issues the required coils, updates inventory records and ensures stock accuracy. Finished coils are later stored until Dispatch arranges shipment.

best_practices: Keep warehouse aisles clean and unobstructed.
important_notes: Inventory accuracy directly affects production planning.
Warehouse safety is as important as inventory management.
Every material movement should be properly documented.
Good housekeeping improves both safety and operational efficiency.
